---
title: "Re-coarsening Loss"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.method-page}
# Re-coarsening Consistency

The generated HR field should remain compatible with the LR information. The re-coarsening loss applies a coarse
operator \(C(\cdot)\) to the predicted HR field:

$$
\mathcal{L}_{coarse}=\|C(\widehat{\mathbf{x}}_{HR})-\mathbf{x}_{LR}\|_2^2.
$$

In OceanLens, this was fine-tuned from `v4_s1_logit_t` to reduce large-scale drift while allowing the FM branch to add
small-scale residual energy.

Related idea: consistency losses used in generative downscaling and CorrDiff-style workflows.
:::
